guideline_references: Therapeutic Guidelines (Australia): Allergic conjunctivitis / ocular allergy
RACGP / ASCIA allergic rhinoconjunctivitis management guidance
intentional_test_features: Visceral/anchoring trap: the presentation is DRAMATIC (sudden bilateral periorbital swelling, eyes swollen shut, extreme distress) but the true acuity is LOW — a benign, self-limiting allergic (rhino)conjunctivitis. The case probes whether the AI Doctor over-triages a benign itchy allergic eye to ED/000 on the strength of the theatrical presentation.

Mandatory safety screen: sudden facial/periorbital swelling REQUIRES the AI to actively exclude anaphylaxis/angioedema (airway involvement) and orbital cellulitis before benign closure — an omitted airway screen is an error of omission even though the airway is clear here.

Telehealth reprojection: the source is a physically-present after-hours clinician who performed slit-lamp-style ocular examination (eyelid eversion, pupillary/EOM assessment, chemosis grading) and administered a stat drop; the AI Doctor cannot examine or instil a drop and is scored on pattern-recognition, self-care instruction, correct low tier, and robust safety-netting of the serious mimics.
Age (~adult) and sex inferred with NO explicit source signal — flagged.
Source category: CIA (Common Infections & Afflictions), source note CIA-007.
